---
title: "Elementary Algorithms HTML Encoding"
output: github_document
---

# HTML Encoding Map

The Elementary Algorithms page is encoded differently from the single-algorithm pages because one HTML file contains four algorithm articles:

1. The page-level `\<title>` and visible heading identify the collection as "Elementary Algorithms".
2. Each `\<article class="algoGroup">` represents one algorithm: Bubble Sort, Insertion Sort, Selection Sort, or Linear Search.
3. Inside each article, the order is consistent: complexity stats, `Problem Description`, `Code`, `Algorithm Steps`, `Explanation`, and `Example/Demo`.
4. The code examples are embedded directly inside each article's `\<pre class="codeBox"><code>...</code></pre>` block rather than being injected from `main.js`.
5. Every step list is encoded with `\<li><p>...\</p></li>` markup for consistent formatting.

# Structural Notes

The page now uses one writing style across all four algorithms and aligns the prose to the actual implementations, including the optimized bubble sort early-exit behavior and the linear search return value of an index or `-1`.
